summary: The diff implements a comprehensive BookStore Community App covering all 10 requirements. Backend (backend/main.mo) implements Book and Order data models with CRUD, admin-only mutations, order placement, and retrieval. Frontend implements Catalog, Book Detail, Cart, Checkout, Order Confirmation, Order History, and Admin Panel pages with routing, auth via Internet Identity, cart context, warm bookshop theme, and responsive navigation. Several pages were omitted from the diff summary due to truncation limits, but their routing registration and imports in App.tsx strongly suggests they exist. Minor gaps noted below based on available evidence.

missing_requirements.requirement: Order Confirmation page must show orderId, list of purchased items with prices, total amount paid, buyer name, contact, and order timestamp; accessible only after successful order submission.
missing_requirements.reason: OrderConfirmationPage is registered as a route in App.tsx and imported, but its implementation file was omitted from the diff due to file limits. Cannot confirm all required fields are displayed or that the page is gated to post-order-success navigation only.

missing_requirements.requirement: Order History page fetching orders from backend by buyer principal, sorted descending by date, with empty state.
missing_requirements.reason: OrderHistoryPage is registered as a route and imported in App.tsx, but its implementation file was omitted from the diff due to file limits. Cannot confirm all ACs including sorting by date descending, empty state message, and per-order item summary display are implemented.

missing_requirements.requirement: Checkout page with name/contact validation, order summary, Place Order call, loading state, and cart clear on success.
missing_requirements.reason: CheckoutPage is registered as a route in App.tsx but its implementation file was omitted from the diff due to file limits. Cannot confirm all ACs are met.

hallucinated_features.description: User Profile setup modal that prompts authenticated users to enter name and contact on first login, stored in the backend as a UserProfile record with dedicated CRUD functions.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention a user profile system, profile setup modal, or storing user profiles separately in the canister. The backend exposes getCallerUserProfile, getUserProfile, and saveCallerUserProfile, and the frontend shows a ProfileSetupModal to new users, none of which was requested.
